title: Call-In API Return Codes

Call-In API Return Codes

Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

The following table describes codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API. Unless noted otherwise, these codes are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror.

Not all call-in API functions can return all of these codes. In some cases, where there are only a few possibilities, we have listed them. In all other cases, we have referred to this table.

35 rows found
Table 1. Call-In API Return Codes: -1 – -49

Value

Meaning

35 rows found
35 rows found

Value

Meaning

0

Success.

-1

An error occurred.

-2

Occurrence or record not found; the table is empty or end of file was encountered.

Occurrence removed since last retrieve.

The entity is painted as an up entity and the key value was not found during the database lookup.

-3

Exceptional I/O error (hardware or software).

-4

The table or file could not be opened. The entity is not painted or the corresponding table or file does not exist in the database.

-5

No write or delete permission for the table or file. The occurrence is read-only (cannot be locked).

-6

An error occurred while writing, updating, or deleting the table or file; for example, lack of disk space, no write permission, or violation of a database constraint.

-7

The key exists in the database but was not found in the hitlist. This occurs when the user tries to enter a duplicate key.

-8

End of hitlist.

-9

DBMS logon error. This can occur if the database connection has been lost or the maximum number of DBMS logons has already been reached.

-10

Occurrence has been modified or removed since it was retrieved; the occurrence should be reloaded.

-11

Occurrence already locked.

-12

An error occurred while trying to read or write to the file.

-14

The entity is painted as a normal down entity and multiple hits were found during the database lookup (ambiguous key).

-15

The entity is painted as an up entity and multiple hits were found during the database lookup.

-16

Network error.

-17

Connection lost.

-18

Application failed to connect to the Uniface Router, or failed to start an exclusive Uniface Server.

-19

Uniface Server exited with fatal error.

-20

Uniface Router could not accept new client, $MAX_CLIENTS exceeded.

-21

Network logon error.

-22

Application failed to register with the Uniface Router.

-23

Registration with Uniface Router specified UST that is already in use.

-24

Router could not start Uniface Server process (executable not found).

-25

Router could not route request to specific Uniface Server process.

-31

No license for requested action. Contact your Uniface representative.

-32

A time-out occurred.

-33

Statement not allowed in batch mode. Use a test on $batch to avoid this.

-34

Changes to the active path not allowed.

-35

A trigger returned a negative value in $status.

-36

Statement not allowed in test mode.

-37

read statement uses an offset option but the database driver does not support pagination

-38

Incomplete or wrong parameters for offset

-40

Not enough memory

-41

Invalid characters in directory or filename

30 rows found
Table 2. Call-In API Return Codes: -50 – -99

Value

Meaning

30 rows found
30 rows found

Value

Meaning

-50

Signature descriptor for the current component not found (in UAR or resource file). For example, the component name provided is not valid.

Signature descriptor is found, but the interface is missing or invalid.

-51

The identifier of the compiled component does not match the identifier in the signature descriptor (in UAR or resource file).

-52

Protocol error (wrong sequence of operations).

-53

An error occurred while copying the occurrences of an entity parameter to occurrences of the activated operation. This occurs at the start of processing for the activate statement on either the client or the server.

-54

An error occurred while copying occurrences of the entity parameter in the activated operation to occurrences of the component instance. This occurs at the end of processing for the activate statement on either the client or the server.

-55

An error occurred while getting an OUT or INOUT parameter from the activated operation. For example, the actual parameter provided cannot be used to receive output because it is a constant string. This occurs at the end of processing for the activate statement on either the client or the server.

-56

An error occurred while putting an IN or INOUT parameter into the activated operation. This error occurs at the start of processing for the activate statement on either the client or the server.

-57

The named instance cannot be found in the component pool.

-58

The named component cannot be found.

A modal form is started using run, and the form is already running.

-59

No definition found for operation.

-60

An attempt was made by an instance other than the current modal form instance to start an operation other than the EXEC operation.

-61

The entity specified as an entity parameter is a dummy entity.

-62

The entity specified as an entity parameter must be the same entity as that specified in the operation. That is, one is a supertype and the other is a subtype of that supertype, or both are subtypes of the same supertype.

-63

No property found.

-64

Operation not mapped.

-65

No implementation found for operation.

-66

Not allowed on occurrence parameter.

-67

Occurrence parameter get error.

-68

Occurrence parameter put error.

-69

Occurrence parameter error. The related occurrence is not available.

-70

Entity descriptor not found.

-71

Error in getting state.

-72

Entity parameter is both source and destination.

-80

Failure to connect to URB.

-81

Failure to connect to engine.

-84

No handle or empty handle specified.

-85

No request specified.

-86

Failure to expose object.

-87

Unsupported or unknown request encountered.

-88

Object has been deleted.

26 rows found
Table 3. Call-In API Return Codes: -100 – -299

Value

Meaning

26 rows found
26 rows found

Value

Meaning

-150

A hardware or software error occurred. Contact your Uniface representative.

-151

An exception error has been thrown.

-154

An instance with this name already exists. This error code can be returned when (for example):

  • A modal form which is already active is activated again

  • An attempt is made to activate a modal form from a non-modal form

-155

An error occurred while creating an instance:

  • An unknown property occurs in the instance properties.

  • A property in the instance properties has a value that is not valid.

  • The component could not be loaded.

  • An exit statement was executed in the operation INIT.

-156

Wrong or undefined implementation.

-159

Message could not be delivered to requested queue.

-160

Unknown communication mode

-161

Illegal mixture of synchronous and asynchronous communication modes.

-162

Deleting the instance has been postponed because the instance is busy.

For example, operation A1 in INSTA activates operation B1 in INSTB. Operation B1, in turn, activates operation A2 in INSTA. Operation A2 performs an exit, but INSTA cannot be deleted until operations B1 and A1 complete.

-163

Deleting the instance has been postponed because the instance has at least one busy child instance. The instance will be deleted when it no longer has busy children.

For example, non-modal form FRMX starts an attached non-modal form FRMY. While the application is idle, the user clicks on FRMY, activating a trigger which sends a message to FRMX. This message results in an exit (on FRMX), but FRMX cannot be deleted until its child instance FRMY is not busy.

-164

The instance is in the process of being deleted.

For example, between a deleteinstance or exit and the time the instance is actually deleted, an attempt is made to activate an operation in the instance being deleted.

-165

Handle not valid.

-166

Component instance could not be created by the newinstance statement because the component is stateless.

-170

Transaction manager failure.

-171

Transaction cannot be transported.

-172

Transaction is not started.

-173

Transaction is not running.

-250

Skeleton file not found or is incorrect.

-251

When $web ="", the output file is not specified or is the same as the skeleton file.

-252

Output file could not be written.

-253

Image file could not be written.

-254

Nested iteration over the same entity.

-255

This statement must be used in a Web run-time environment ($web !="").

-256

No further input expected, because no fill-out document has been generated.

-257

One or more fields cannot be accessed; that is, field names referenced in the fill-out document skeleton file are not available in the current Uniface form.

-258

Form synchronization error, that is, the user has submitted an HTML document that does not correspond to the last one generated. It is, instead, a form generated by Uniface earlier in the same session.
Table 4. Call-In API Return Codes: -300 – -999

Value

Meaning

-300

An error in declarative syntax occurred.

-301

Violation of restricted delete constraint.

-302

A key field contains a profile character or the key is incomplete.

-303

A key field is empty.

-350

Unable to start help.

-351

Platform does not support native help. (In this case you may want to use the help [native] statement to provide help information.)

-352

Unable to map logical name.

-353

Requested help topic or keyword not found.

-354

Native help does not support the requested option.

-400

Uniface could not print, for example:

  • Printing is already being performed ($printing =1).

  • ^QUIT was used in the Print form.

  • The print mode is not valid. For more information on printmodes, see print

-401

An error occurred during printbreak:

  • Uniface is not printing ($printing =0).

  • printbreak encountered in a header or trailer frame.

-403

Nonexistent field in a u_where clause.

-404

The TRX-formatted DML statement from a where clause or an sql statement exceeds 32 KB.

-405

Modal form has focus.

-406

An error occurred during a filebox statement.
Table 5. Call-In API Return Codes: -1000 – -1099

Value

Meaning

-1001

Not a valid String value.

-1002

Not a valid Numeric value.

-1003

Not a valid Float value.

-1004

Not a valid Date value.

-1005

Not a valid Time value.

-1006

Not a valid Datetime value.

-1007

Not a valid Raw value.

-1008

Not a valid Boolean value.

-1009

Not a valid Linear Date value.

-1010

Not a valid Linear Time value.

-1011

Not a valid Linear Datetime value.

-1012

Not a valid Image value.

-1013

Not a valid syntax string.

28 rows found
Table 6. Call-In API Return Codes: -1100 – -1199

Value

Meaning

28 rows found
28 rows found

Value

Meaning

-1101

An incorrect field name was provided; either the field name is not valid syntactically or the field is not available in the component.

-1102

The entity name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component.

-1103

The model name provided is not a valid name or the entity is not painted on the component.

-1104

The key number provided is not valid; for example, the key number was out of range.

-1105

The instance name provided is not valid. For more information, see newinstance. For example, the argument contains incorrect characters.

-1106

The component name provided is not valid; for example, the argument contains an empty string ("").

-1107

The path name is not correct or the path does not exist, for example, no assignment is found for the path.

-1109

The entry name specified cannot be found.

-1110

Topic name not known.

-1111

The message identifier is not valid; the field or variable was not found, or the string is not a valid message identifier.

-1112

Option not valid.

-1113

Parameter name not valid or not defined.

-1114

Local variable name not valid or not defined.

-1115

Component variable name not valid or not found.

-1116

General variable not valid.

-1117

Global variable name not valid or not found.

-1118

The argument specified is incorrect.

-1119

ProcScript function not valid.

-1120

The operation name provided is not valid. For more information, see Operation.

-1121

The requested 3GL function was not found.

-1122

Wrong number of arguments.

-1123

Wrong number of parameters.

-1124

Application name not valid.

-1125

The specified menu does not exist.

-1126

A property is not valid.

-1127

Data type not valid.

-1128

The key number specified is an index.

-1129

Item not found.

28 rows found
Table 7. Call-In API Return Codes: -1200 – -1399

Value

Meaning

28 rows found
28 rows found

Value

Meaning

-1201

Value too small.

-1202

Value too large.

-1203

Value out of range.

-1204

Negative value not allowed.

-1205

Zero value not allowed.

-1206

Not an integer.

-1207

Underflow.

-1208

Overflow.

-1209

Divide by zero.

-1301

Syntax error.

-1302

Function not allowed on service.

-1303

Function not allowed on report.

-1304

Function not allowed, unknown context.

-1305

Expression not allowed.

-1306

Condition not allowed.

-1307

Extraction expression is a condition.

-1308

Indirection followed by brackets.

-1309

Operand followed by parentheses.

-1310

Operand followed by square brackets.

-1311

A field, parameter, or variable could not be found in current context.

-1312

The label is not valid.

-1313

The switch is not valid.

-1314

The operand cannot be used as destination.

-1315

The operator is not valid.

-1316

Conditional operators are not allowed in expressions.

-1317

The switch combination is not valid.

-1318

This is not allowed in a self-contained component.

-1319

Function not allowed on server page.

22 rows found
Table 8. Call-In API Return Codes: -1400 – -1699

Value

Meaning

22 rows found
22 rows found

Value

Meaning

-1401

Prompted field not valid.

-1402

Statement not allowed in this trigger.

-1403

Operand not valid.

-1404

Not printing (that is, $printing=0).

-1406

Memory allocation failure.

-1407

Operand is read-only.

-1408

GUI driver not active.

-1409

Not in interactive mode.

-1410

A property has been assigned an incorrect value.

-1411

Edit is not allowed in interactive mode.

-1412

Invalid use of a handle.

-1413

Destination availability scope is too wide.

-1414

Thread start failure.

-1498

Handle is not allowed.

-1499

The specified handle is not valid.

-1500

A DTD could not be located.

-1501

There is a syntax error in the DTD.

-1502

An error occurred during generation of an XML stream.

-1503

An error occurred during parsing of an XML stream.

-1504

An error occurred during validation of an XML stream.

-1505

An error occurred during transformation of an XML stream.

-1506

A mandatory field is not available in the field list of the entity. In the DTD, such fields must be followed by a question mark (?).
